category_sources: source_facts.description says JuiceFS is a cloud-based distributed POSIX file system built on Redis and S3; official GitHub README describes a high-performance POSIX file system for cloud-native environments.
display_name_sources: Official homepage and documentation use the product name JuiceFS; source_facts.name is juicefs.
docs_sources: Official GitHub README links to the JuiceFS Document Center on juicefs.com; dedicated official docs domain/path is available.
repo_sources: source_facts.repo already contains https://github.com/juicedata/juicefs, so repo is returned as null per instruction.
tags_sources: source_facts.description identifies POSIX, Redis, and S3; official GitHub README documents object storage, metadata engines, Hadoop compatibility, S3 gateway, and Kubernetes CSI support.
